examples.title: DFA Simulation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to simulate DFA on input string
examples.explanation: This algorithm simulates any DFA by following transitions for each input symbol. The current state is updated based on the transition function. At the end, acceptance depends on whether the final state is in the set of accepting states.

examples.title: NFA to DFA Conversion (Subset Construction)
examples.explanation: The subset construction converts NFA to DFA by tracking sets of NFA states. The ε-closure computes all states reachable via ε-transitions. In worst case, the resulting DFA has 2^n states.

examples.title: Turing Machine for palindrome recognition
examples.description: TM that accepts strings of form ww^R
examples.explanation: This TM checks palindromes by marking matched pairs from outside. It uses special symbol X to mark visited positions. Time complexity is O(n²) as each symbol may require full traversal.

examples.title: NP-Complete Reduction Example (3-SAT to Clique)
examples.description: Polynomial reduction from 3-SAT to Clique problem
examples.explanation: This reduction proves Clique is NP-hard. The construction ensures: a satisfying assignment maps to a clique (choose true literals), and a clique of size k maps to a satisfying assignment (one literal per clause, no contradictions). This is polynomial (O(m²)) thus CLIQUE ≥p 3-SAT.
